description:
You are a compliance analyst who has just received two raw event‐capture files in CSV
    format under the directory “/home/user/compliance/raw_logs/”.

    Existing input files (already on disk):
      1. /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/login_events.csv
      2. /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/file_access.csv

    Your objectives are:

    1. Create the directory “/home/user/compliance/audit/” (if it does not exist).
    2. Convert the two CSV sources into ONE time-ordered JSON-Lines document called
          /home/user/compliance/audit/unified_events.jsonl
       The rules for every JSON object are very strict:
         • Fields and order inside each object must be exactly:
              event_id, timestamp, user, type, details
         • “type” must be the literal string “login” or “file_access”.
         • “details” is itself a JSON object:
             – If type == "login":  details must contain
                   { "action": "<login|logout>",
                     "source_ip": "<IPv4 address>" }
             – If type == "file_access": details must contain
                   { "file_path": "<full path>",
                     "operation": "<read|write>" }
         • Each JSON object must occupy a single physical line
           (newline-delimited, no trailing commas, no pretty printing).
         • The entire file must be globally sorted by the value of the “timestamp”
           field in ascending ISO-8601 order across both data sources.

    3. Build a CSV summary named
          /home/user/compliance/audit/summary_by_user.csv
       with the exact header:
          user,total_logins,total_logouts,total_reads,total_writes
       For every unique user present in the unified data set output ONE row whose
       integer columns are the counts of each event subtype.

    4. Produce a two-line generation log called
          /home/user/compliance/audit/audit_generation.log
       whose precise format is:

          unified_events.jsonl generated with <N> records
          summary_by_user.csv generated with <M> users

       where <N> is the total number of JSON objects actually written and <M>
       is the number of user rows (excluding the header) in the summary CSV.

    5. Do not alter the original CSV files; work only inside the audit directory.

    Success criteria that the automated checker will validate:

      • The directory “/home/user/compliance/audit/” exists.
      • unified_events.jsonl exists, is newline-delimited JSON and is strictly
        sorted by “timestamp”.
      • Every object in unified_events.jsonl contains exactly the five fields
        in the specified order and embeds a “details” object matching the rules.
      • summary_by_user.csv contains the correct header and the right numeric
        counts for every user found in the raw data.
      • audit_generation.log exists and its two lines match the required
        template literally, with accurate <N> and <M> values.
truth:
# ----------  Input fixture (already present before the task starts) ----------
    # Directory structure
    /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/               (0755)
    /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/login_events.csv  (0644)
    /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/file_access.csv   (0644)

    # Contents of /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/login_events.csv
    event_id,timestamp,user,action,source_ip
    LE1,2023-04-01T09:15:27Z,alice,login,192.168.1.10
    LE2,2023-04-01T12:47:03Z,bob,login,192.168.1.11
    LE3,2023-04-01T17:05:44Z,alice,logout,192.168.1.10
    LE4,2023-04-02T08:01:09Z,charlie,login,192.168.1.12
    LE5,2023-04-02T11:30:17Z,bob,logout,192.168.1.11

    # Contents of /home/user/compliance/raw_logs/file_access.csv
    event_id,timestamp,user,file_path,operation
    FA1,2023-04-01T10:03:12Z,alice,/srv/docs/confidential.pdf,read
    FA2,2023-04-01T10:15:55Z,alice,/srv/docs/confidential.pdf,write
    FA3,2023-04-01T13:22:40Z,bob,/srv/spreadsheets/budget.xlsx,read
    FA4,2023-04-02T09:12:06Z,charlie,/srv/scripts/deploy.sh,read
    FA5,2023-04-02T09:45:55Z,charlie,/srv/scripts/deploy.sh,write
    FA6,2023-04-02T12:11:33Z,bob,/srv/spreadsheets/budget.xlsx,write

    # ----------  Expected output after agent completes the task ----------
    # Directories
    /home/user/compliance/audit/                (0755)

    # 1. /home/user/compliance/audit/unified_events.jsonl (0644)
    #    11 single-line JSON objects, exactly in the order shown below:
    {"event_id":"LE1","timestamp":"2023-04-01T09:15:27Z","user":"alice","type":"login","details":{"action":"login","source_ip":"192.168.1.10"}}
    {"event_id":"FA1","timestamp":"2023-04-01T10:03:12Z","user":"alice","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/docs/confidential.pdf","operation":"read"}}
    {"event_id":"FA2","timestamp":"2023-04-01T10:15:55Z","user":"alice","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/docs/confidential.pdf","operation":"write"}}
    {"event_id":"LE2","timestamp":"2023-04-01T12:47:03Z","user":"bob","type":"login","details":{"action":"login","source_ip":"192.168.1.11"}}
    {"event_id":"FA3","timestamp":"2023-04-01T13:22:40Z","user":"bob","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/spreadsheets/budget.xlsx","operation":"read"}}
    {"event_id":"LE3","timestamp":"2023-04-01T17:05:44Z","user":"alice","type":"login","details":{"action":"logout","source_ip":"192.168.1.10"}}
    {"event_id":"LE4","timestamp":"2023-04-02T08:01:09Z","user":"charlie","type":"login","details":{"action":"login","source_ip":"192.168.1.12"}}
    {"event_id":"FA4","timestamp":"2023-04-02T09:12:06Z","user":"charlie","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/scripts/deploy.sh","operation":"read"}}
    {"event_id":"FA5","timestamp":"2023-04-02T09:45:55Z","user":"charlie","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/scripts/deploy.sh","operation":"write"}}
    {"event_id":"LE5","timestamp":"2023-04-02T11:30:17Z","user":"bob","type":"login","details":{"action":"logout","source_ip":"192.168.1.11"}}
    {"event_id":"FA6","timestamp":"2023-04-02T12:11:33Z","user":"bob","type":"file_access","details":{"file_path":"/srv/spreadsheets/budget.xlsx","operation":"write"}}

    # 2. /home/user/compliance/audit/summary_by_user.csv (0644)
    user,total_logins,total_logouts,total_reads,total_writes
    alice,1,1,1,1
    bob,1,1,1,1
    charlie,1,0,1,1

    # 3. /home/user/compliance/audit/audit_generation.log (0644)
    unified_events.jsonl generated with 11 records
    summary_by_user.csv generated with 3 users
